title: Repair relocatable pkg-config metadata for static link order
prompt: The Parcel C library installs both a self-contained shared library and a set of static archives. Its installed parcel.pc metadata currently breaks static downstream consumers because the private helper archives are emitted in the wrong dependency order: libparcel calls libseal, and libseal calls libhash. Repair the installed pkg-config metadata so ordinary `pkg-config --libs parcel` exposes only the public libparcel link, while `pkg-config --static --libs parcel` adds the private helpers after it in a one-pass-linker-safe order. Preserve the relative order of every link item. Keep the package relocatable by deriving its prefix from pcfiledir; do not bake the configure-time prefix, DESTDIR, or a test path into the metadata. Both shared and forced-static downstream C17 consumers must compile cleanly with warnings as errors and run successfully after the installed prefix is moved. Do not change the public API, library implementation, build products, or tests. All verification must remain offline.
